page_0511_0.png: Condition Scripts editor in AVEVA InTouch showing the $InactivityWarning tag condition set to 'On True'. The script body reads: IF $Operator <> "None" THEN Show "Inactivity"; ENDIF; — This opens the Inactivity warning window when an operator is logged in and inactivity is detected.
page_0511_1.png: Condition Scripts editor with the Condition Type dropdown open, showing trigger options: On True (selected), While True, While False. The $InactivityWarning tag is configured to trigger the script when the inactivity warning becomes active.
page_0512_0.png: Condition Scripts editor configured for $InactivityWarning with Condition Type set to 'On False'. The script body contains: Hide "Inactivity"; — This hides the Inactivity warning window when the user interacts (clearing the inactivity state).
page_0512_1.png: WindowMaker Scripts pane showing the hierarchical tree of script categories: Application, Key, Condition (1 script: $InactivityWarning), Data change, QuickFunctions, and ActiveX event. The Condition category is expanded showing the single condition-triggered script.
page_0513.png: InTouch runtime Inactivity Warning screen — a bright red alert window with bold black text 'Inactivity Warning' and yellow instruction text 'Click mouse or use keyboard to prevent system log off'. This appears after 15 seconds of user inactivity.
page_0514_0.png: InTouch runtime showing the Inactivity Timeout screen — red background with 'Inactivity Timeout' title and yellow text 'You have been logged off!' indicating the automatic logoff has occurred after the timeout period.
page_0514_1.png: InTouch runtime Logon panel showing User: None, User Full Name: None, Access Level: 0, with Logon and Logoff buttons — confirming the user was logged off after inactivity timeout.
page_0515.png: WindowViewer configuration dialog (Preferences tab) showing Inactivity settings: Warning set to 0, Timeout set to 0 (disabling inactivity timeouts for testing), and Minimum access level set to 9999. Also shows Startup as icon and Enable tag viewer checkboxes (both unchecked).
page_0516.png: AVEVA InTouch backstage File menu with the Configure option selected, showing sub-options: Security, WindowMaker, WindowViewer (highlighted), Distributed name manager, Historical logging, and Alarms — navigating to WindowViewer configuration.
page_0517.png: Window Properties dialog for creating a new popup window named 'MenuBarCover' with Comment 'Prevents clicking the menu bar'. Properties: Window type=Popup, X=0, Y=-20, Width=1920, Height=20, Window color=White, Title bar unchecked, Frame style=None — a narrow overlay designed to block the menu bar area.
page_0518.png: WindowMaker development environment with the Data Change Scripts dialog open. The Scripts pane shows Data change selected, with the Windows tab listing project windows including AlarmDisplay, Carousel, ContentDisplay, MenuBarCover, Navigation, and OverviewDisplay. The process overview screen is visible in the background.
page_0519.png: Data Change Scripts editor with Tagname set to $AccessLevel. The script reads: { Menu Access } IF $AccessLevel > 5000 THEN { Allow Menu Access } Hide "MenuBarCover"; ELSE { Deny Menu Access } Show "MenuBarCover"; ENDIF; — Controls menu bar visibility based on user access level.
page_0520_0.png: WindowViewer configuration dialog (Startup tab) showing Home Windows thumbnails: AlarmDisplay, Carousel, ContentDisplay, Inactivity, Logon, MenuBarCover, Navigation, and OverviewDisplay — all selected as startup windows for the runtime application.
page_0520_1.png: WindowMaker confirmation dialog with blue information icon stating 'WindowViewer configuration successfully updated.' — confirming the Home Windows startup configuration was saved.
page_0521_0.png: WindowViewer runtime showing the Mixer View application (TRAININGGALAXY-SMIXERVIEW) with the menu bar covered/hidden. Navigation buttons visible (Mixers, Alarm Overview, Historical Alarms, Historian Client Trend). User panel shows User: None, Access Level: 0 — demonstrating the MenuBarCover is active for non-admin users.
page_0521_1.png: WindowViewer runtime showing the same Mixer View application with administrator logged in (Access Level: 9999). The File/Logic/Special menu bar is now visible because the MenuBarCover was hidden. Alarm table shows 6 of 26 Line1 alarms including communication failures, level alarms, and agitator speed alarms.
page_0522.png: Data Change Scripts editor with $AccessLevel tag, showing the updated script with EnableDisableKeys() function: IF $AccessLevel > 5000 THEN Hide "MenuBarCover"; EnableDisableKeys(0,0,0); ELSE Show "MenuBarCover"; EnableDisableKeys(1,1,1); ENDIF; — Disables Alt, Esc, and Win keys for low-access users.
page_0523_0.png: Application Script editor with Condition Type dropdown open showing three options: While Running, On Startup (highlighted/selected), and On Shutdown — selecting the startup trigger for a new application-level script. Scripts used: 0.
page_0523_1.png: Application Script editor with Condition Type set to 'On Startup'. The script reads: { Disable the Alt, Esc, Win keys } EnableDisableKeys(1,1,1); — This runs once at application startup to disable keyboard shortcuts for security lockout.
page_0524.png: WindowViewer File menu open in the runtime application, showing menu options: Open Window..., Close Window..., WindowMaker..., Exit (highlighted), and About WindowViewer... — demonstrating that the File menu is accessible (menu bar not covered) for testing key combinations.
page_0525_0.png: Windows Task Manager showing three running AVEVA applications: InTouch HMI WindowMaker (32 bit), InTouch HMI WindowViewer (32 bit), and System Platform IDE (32 bit) — confirming all three core development and runtime tools are running simultaneously.
page_0525_1.png: Windows 10 Start Menu showing the All Apps list with AVEVA suite installed: AVEVA Documentation, GRAccess Toolkit, Historian, InTouch Access Anywhere, InTouch HMI, System Monitor, and System Platform. Pinned tiles include System Platform IDE, InTouch HMI Web-Client, Application Manager, Trend, and Historian Client Web.
page_0526.png: Final page of Lab 22 — the end of the Runtime Customization lab. Shows the conclusion of testing EnableDisableKeys() function with administrator login, and the End of Lab marker.
